[[Category:Ringworld]]
[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]
[[File:Cat genders in Old Aelaki.svg|thumb]]
== Overview ==
Aelaki (also written Allochy) is the language of the Kxakxa people. It is famous for its '''non-concatenative morphology''' — grammatical meaning is encoded by reshaping a triconsonantal (occasionally quadri-) root rather than by stringing affixes.

Three phonological operations lie at the heart of the system:
{| class="wikitable"
! Operation !! Form !! Core meaning
|-
| '''Base''' || C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃ || singular / perfective-incomplete / positive degree
|-
| '''Reduplication''' || C₁V₁C₂C₂V₂C₃ || partitive plural / imperfective / comparative
|-
| '''Umlaut''' || C₁(front-V₁)C₂(front-V₂)C₃ || collective / perfective-completed / superlative
|-
| '''Zero-infix''' /f/ || C₁V₁fC₂V₂C₃ || negation / zero quantity
|}

The zero-/f/ infix never appears in the yes/no particles Su/Fu. It is restricted to content words and a handful of linking particles such as '''zuf''' "and … not".

Example with verb root d-p-z "shoot":
* '''Dapaz''' "shot at (once, success not implied)"
* '''Dapapaz''' "shooting at (ongoing)"
* '''Dæpæz''' "shot (single hit achieved)"
* '''Dapfaz''' "did not shoot / no shots fired"

== Modern grammar ==
=== Gender system ===
Aelaki distinguishes four [[Category:Grammatical genders|grammatical genders]]: [[Child]], [[Female]], [[Male]], and [[Inanimate]]. Speakers are sequential hermaphrodites (child → female → male).

=== Case system ===
Five [[Category:Cases|cases]]: [[Agent]], [[Patient]], [[Instrumental]], [[Dative]], and [[Speaker]], plus [[Possessive]] constructions (inalienable and alienable).

=== Tense-evidential system ===
Six temporal frames ([[Past]], [[Mythic]], [[Hesternal]], [[Present]], [[Hodiernal]], [[Crastinal]], [[Future]]) cross-cut by evidentials ([[Visual]], [[Auditory]], [[Inferential]]) and moods ([[Imperative]], [[Optative]], [[Deliberative]]).

=== Aspect ===
Core aspects include [[Habitual]], [[Continuous]], [[Gnomic]], [[Telic]], and [[Atelic]]. Stative verbs use prefix aspects: [[Inchoative]] (nü-), [[Cessative]] (nï-), [[Resumptive]] (lu-), and [[Almost]] (ko-).

=== Number ===
[[Singular]], [[Plural|Partitive plural]], [[Collective]], and [[Zero]] (negation/questions).

=== Parts of speech ===
[[Adjective]]s, [[Adverb]]s, [[Adpositions]], [[Numerals]], and [[Converbs]] each have their own morphological systems derived from the core templatic operations.

== Historical development ==
The prefix uchi- (go) is often appended to words to make them more regular later in the history.

== Shifts in chronological order ==

# classifiers -> gender
# Evidentiality verb development and verb agreement
# SVO order frees up

== Earliest grammar ==
Used classifiers

Copula was irregular

== Irregular proto language copula ==
Ghi was standard copula

Gi was irregular copula

Originally yes or no sentences were asked in the english style

"Are you sick":

"You are sick"

Gi was the second person version

When word order began to free up Gi began to become a yes-no question particle

== Proto language ==
Used a system of classifiers like Japanese. 

Classifiers got fused in

Classifiers are still used but a lot less frequently
Genders

# i (child)
## -bi (child classifier)
# e (female)
## -d'e (woman classifier)
# ae (male)
## -aeki (old/male classifier)
## aek is a more common suffix, aeki is pretty rare

=== Gender development example ===
eub bi -> eubi 

(child)

=== Demonstrative development ===
dzhih bi ->dzhib

(this child) -> this (child)

dzhih d'e -> dzhed'

(this woman) -> this (female)

dzhih aeki -> dzhaek

(this man) -> this (male)

=== Pronoun development ===

==== First person ====
zun bi -> zim

zun d'e -> zen

zun aeki -> zaeng

==== Second person ====
t'i bi -> t'ib

t'i d'e -> t'et

t'i aeki -> t'aek

==== Third person ====
she bi -> shib

she d'e -> shed'

she aeki -> shaek

==== Fourth person ====
fo bi -> fibi

fo d'e -> fed'e

fo aeki -> faek

=== Pronoun plural forms ===

==== First person ====
zun bi bi -> zimbib 

zun d'ede -> zend'ed 

zun aekiki -> zaengkik -> zaengkaek

==== Second person ====
t'i bibi -> t'ibib 

t'i d'ede > t'etet 

t'i aekiki -> t'aekik -> t'aekaek

==== Third person ====
she bibi -> shibib -> 

she d'ede -> shed'ed -> 

she aekiki -> shaekik -> shaekaek

==== Fourth person ====
fo bi -> fifibi

fo d'e -> fefed'e

fo aeki -> faefaek

=== Pronoun collective forms ===

==== First person ====
zun bel bi -> zimbil 

zun bel d'e -> zed'el 

zun bel aeki -> zaekel 

==== Second person ====
t'i bel bi -> t'ibil 

t'i bel d'e -> t'etel 

t'i bel aeki -> t'aekael 

==== Third person ====
she bel bi -> shibil 

she bel d'e -> shed'el -> shedel

she bel aeki -> shaekael  

==== Fourth person ====
fo bel bi -> fibil

fo bel d'e -> fed'el

fo bel aeki -> faekael

=== Number development ===
originally base 8

first 8 numbers have highly obscure meanings

9 means new (pie reference)

10 b'el means two hands derived from earlier form b'ebel

11 m'akx means extra, one more than ten

12 wiv means rings. 

50 (60d) op'iv means complete 12, from the earlier point of base 60

100 (120d) bolop'iv means two sixties, from the earlier point of base 60

=== Pluralization development ===
Pluralization has two forms

plural and collective

plural is determined by reduplication

-bel was originally a dual form, from the word bel meaning two

=== Plural verbs ===
In the proto language reduplication of a verb indicated a frequentative form of the verb, but it got merged in with 

collective verbs developed from a dual form of the verb that analogously did that

=== Copula development ===
Original copula is ghi derived from a word for "hold"

=== Word order and verb agreement development ===
Initial word order was SVO, direct-inverse alignment

inverse marker na comes from a word meaning to stand still

person hierarchy
{| class="wikitable"
|+
!Person
|-
|I (male)
|-
|I (female)
|-
|I (child)
|-
|You (male)
|-
|You (female)
|-
|You (child)
|-
|3rd person male
|-
|3rd person female
|-
|3rd person child
|-
|3rd person neuter
|}

=== First person child ===
Zun bi bvae sugh -> Zim Bivae Sugh

I drink water 

Zun bi na bvae sugh -> Zimna Bivae Sugh

Water drinks me

(note that this formulation may be wrong if I decide to place second person higher in the hierarchy than first person)

Zun bi bha t'i aeki -> Zim Bibha T'aek -> Zimbibhat'aek -> Zibhat'ae

I see you

Zun bi na bha t'i aeki -> Zim Binabha T'aek -> Zinabhat'ae

You see me

=== First person female ===
Zun d'e bvae sugh -> Zen Bevae Sugh

I drink water 

Zun d'e na bvae sugh -> Zena Bevae Sugh

Water drinks me

(note that this formulation may be wrong if I decide to place second person higher in the hierarchy than first person)

Zun d'e bha t'i aeki -> Zen Bibha T'aek -> Zimbibhat'aek -> Zibhat'ae

I see you

Zun d'e na bha t'i aeki -> Zena bha T'aek -> Zinabhat'ae

You see me

=== First person male ===
Zun aeki bvae sugh -> Zaeng Baevae Sugh

I drink water 

Zun aeki na bvae sugh -> Zaengna Baevae Sugh

Water drinks me

(note that this formulation may be wrong if I decide to place second person higher in the hierarchy than first person)

== Sound shifts ==

# the great l shift
## l- -> l-
## -l- -> -ʎ- 
## -l -> -i

== Example shift ==
Sak -> Sak -> Sak -> Sak -> Sak

Sakel -> Sakei -> Saki -> Soki -> Sok

Saksak -> Sasak -> Sasak

== Proto-Aelaki verb reference ==

A working list of proto-Aelaki verb roots, kept as a seed for diachronic work. Most modern lexical entries derive from these by the sound shifts above. The list is intentionally Swadesh-style — concrete, frequent verbs first.

=== Worked paradigm: ''aech'' "to see" ===

The one verb whose full proto paradigm has been worked out. ''Zun'' is the 1st-person root; ''-aech-'' is the bare verb; ''-el'' is the collective; doubled ''-aech'' is the plural; ''zel-'' introduces an inclusive 1st+2nd person form.

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Singular !! Plural !! Collective
|-
| 1sg || ''Zaech'' || ''Zaechaech'' || ''Zaechel''
|-
| 1+2 incl. || ''Zelaech'' || ''Zelaechaech'' || ''Zelaechel''
|-
| 2 || ''t'aech'' || ''t'aechaech'' || ''t'aechel''
|-
| 3 || ''aech'' || ''aechaech'' || ''aechel''
|}

=== Verb root list ===

{| class="wikitable sortable"
! English !! Proto-Aelaki root
|-
| to see || aech
|-
| to walk || ael
|-
| to hear || eukxeu
|-
| to eat || kihggx
|-
| to drink || kxop'u
|-
| to speak || rod'
|-
| to run || exu
|-
| to sleep || on'i
|-
| to wake || aegx
|-
| to sit || ek
|-
| to stand || gxot'
|-
| to read || seugxeu
|-
| to write || aeb'
|-
| to give || es
|-
| to take || bvukx
|-
| to find || eudzha
|-
| to lose || dhungu
|-
| to throw || ak
|-
| to catch || og
|-
| to push || okx
|-
| to pull || ihngl'
|-
| to open || igl'ae
|-
| to close || t'ipfu
|-
| to start || d'angl'e
|-
| to stop || euni
|-
| to love || ihngl'ih
|-
| to hate || saez
|-
| to fear || rubvo
|-
| to hope || chidh
|-
| to remember || ux
|-
| to forget || od
|-
| to know || ihg
|-
| to think || bvez
|-
| to understand || pfeukl'i
|-
| to learn || ged
|-
| to teach || kxagx
|-
| to help || ibv
|-
| to work || ange
|-
| to play || euzae
|-
| to build || ed
|-
| to destroy || neb'u
|-
| to grow || ihdho
|-
| to cut || en'
|-
| to clean || kl'et'
|-
| to wash || ova
|-
| to cook || okl'eu
|-
| to bake || dzhihpf
|-
| to freeze || togxu
|-
| to melt || dzhaz
|-
| to draw || n'ihz
|-
| to paint || ngl'igl'
|-
| to sing || aep'
|-
| to dance || ingi
|-
| to listen || engl'
|-
| to shout || ihm'
|-
| to whisper || pfangae
|-
| to ask || aen
|-
| to answer || aengl'ih
|-
| to say || daeda
|-
| to tell || ighe
|-
| to call || ngaech
|-
| to need || kxokx
|-
| to want || kl'azi
|-
| to use || b'upf
|-
| to have || saelu
|-
| to own || ax
|-
| to buy || dhon'
|-
| to sell || dzhigl'eu
|-
| to pay || ngl'eku
|-
| to lend || oti
|-
| to borrow || wihb'
|-
| to show || idh
|-
| to hide || ag
|-
| to come || eucheu
|-
| to go || aedzh
|-
| to leave || okl'u
|-
| to return || veugx
|-
| to arrive || b'ex
|-
| to stay || ngl'obvu
|-
| to fall || odzhu
|-
| to rise || edzh
|-
| to lie (down) || eudzhih
|-
| to climb || in'ih
|-
| to swim || agh
|-
| to fly || ghun'
|-
| to travel || ihdzhe
|-
| to drive || fota
|-
| to ride || bvin'ae
|-
| to carry || aen'
|-
| to lift || okxeu
|-
| to drop || ukx
|-
| to catch (again) || epf
|-
| to throw (again) || gxot'i
|-
| to hit || gaekx
|-
| to kick || et'e
|-
| to break || rom'
|-
| to repair || t'ase
|-
| to create || dhukeu
|-
| to kill || waekl'o
|}

== See also ==

* [[Phonology]], [[Noun]], [[Verb]], [[Adjective]] — core grammar pages
* [[Basic syntax]], [[Word order]] — sentence structure
* [[Test sentences]], [[Random sentences]] — worked example sentences
* [[Switch references]] — clause-chain topic management
* [[Aelaki Lexicon]], [[Swadesh]] — word lists
* [[Case system]] — case overview
* [[Contact influence]], [[Illish pronouns]] — language contact
* [[The divine 60]] — sexagenary cycle used in the culture
* [[Git commit log]] — repository change log reflected into the wiki

[[Category:git synced pages to audit]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
